skills.name: Agent Evaluation
skills.description: Holistically evaluates an AI agent's performance on multi-step tasks by running standardised benchmarks, scoring full interaction trajectories, and producing reproducible capability reports.

evidence.notes: Agent-SafetyBench (2024) -- benchmark with 349 interaction environments and 2,000 test cases covering safety risks in agent deployments; establishes reproducible trajectory-level metrics.
TruLens -- open-source evaluation and tracking for LLM experiments and AI agents; CI, active maintenance, 5k+ stars.
Giskard OSS -- testing library for LLM agents with automated vulnerability scanning and RAG evaluation; Apache-2 license.

skills.name: Agentic Workflow Design
skills.description: Autonomously designs, authors, and iterates on complex multi-step workflows by translating high-level goals into executable DAGs or playbooks, managing task dependencies and distributed execution environments.
skills.conditions: Requires a workflow orchestration target (e.g. Airflow, Temporal) and access to its authoring API.

evidence.notes: Airflow AGENTS.md defines agentic workflow design patterns using the TaskFlow API for dynamic DAG generation.

skills.name: API Call
skills.description: Constructs, executes, and handles responses from HTTP APIs by interpreting documentation and selecting appropriate endpoints and parameters.

evidence.notes: Gorilla (Patil et al.) â€” LLM that generates accurate API calls across TorchHub, TensorFlow Hub, and HuggingFace; 20.43% AST accuracy improvement over GPT-4.

skills.name: Architecture Diagram
skills.description: Generates technical architecture, infrastructure, or cloud diagrams as structured visual artifacts from natural-language system descriptions.
skills.conditions: Requires enough system context to identify components, relationships, boundaries, and rendering constraints.

evidence.notes: Hermes Agent architecture-diagram skill generates standalone HTML files with inline SVG architecture diagrams from system descriptions.

skills.name: Audience Model
skills.description: Adapts tone, complexity, and framing of output to match a target audience profile.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Audience Model.

skills.name: Automated Testing
skills.description: Generates test suites, executes them in a sandbox, interprets failures, and iterates until the target pass rate is reached.

evidence.notes: SWE-bench Verified â€” open-source evaluation harness where agents fix GitHub issues by generating and passing test suites; full execution logs archived.

skills.name: Autonomous Data Scientist
skills.description: Conducts end-to-end data science workflows autonomously: hypothesis generation, data analysis, statistical modeling, and insight reporting.
skills.conditions: Requires dataset access and compute environment. Minimum 3 Class A/B evidence sources.

evidence.notes: DS-1000 â€” benchmark of 1000 data science problems across NumPy/Pandas/Scikit-learn; GPT-4 achieves 43.3% pass@1, establishing reproducible DS evaluation.
AutoML Survey (He et al., ACM CSUR) â€” systematic review of autonomous ML pipeline search; demonstrates automated feature engineering, model selection, and HPO.
AutoGen (Microsoft) â€” multi-agent framework with reproducible data science notebooks; science agent achieves 44.3% on DS-1000 with full execution logs.

skills.description: Navigates web pages, fills forms, clicks elements, and extracts information by combining web search with screenshot-based GUI control to complete multi-step web tasks.

WebArena â€” self-hosted web environment with 812 realistic tasks; reproducible benchmark with ground-truth evaluation scripts.

SkillsMP top hit: sickn33/antigravity-awesome-skills (35k+ stars), multiple community variants including context-compression-v2.

HuggingFace PEFT -- state-of-the-art LoRA/QLoRA/IA3 fine-tuning; 100k+ stars, CI, active maintenance.
LlamaFactory (ACL 2024) -- unified efficient fine-tuning of 100+ LLMs and VLMs; 50k+ stars, reproducible benchmarks.

skills.name: Grill Me

skills.title: The Domain Sovereign

NeMo Guardrails -- open-source toolkit for adding programmable safety rails to LLM-based conversational systems; actively maintained.

skills.name: Multimodal Reasoning
skills.description: Integrates information from images and text to answer questions requiring visual grounding and logical inference across modalities.
skills.conditions: Requires vision-language model capability.

evidence.notes: LLaVA â€” large language and vision assistant; 85.1% on ScienceQA and 64.3% on TextVQA, establishing reproducible multimodal reasoning benchmark.

skills.name: Object Detection
skills.description: Locates and classifies multiple objects within images by producing bounding boxes, confidence scores, and category labels in a single forward pass.

evidence.notes: DETR (Carion et al., 2020) -- end-to-end object detection with transformers; eliminates hand-crafted anchor design and achieves COCO AP 42.0 with ResNet-50, matching Faster R-CNN with simplified pipeline.
Ultralytics YOLOv8 -- production real-time detection library; COCO AP 50.2 at 8ms inference, 25k+ GitHub stars, reproducible training and evaluation scripts for detection and segmentation.

skills.name: OCR
skills.description: Extracts machine-readable text from raster images, scanned pages, and photo documents using optical character recognition, preserving layout and handling skew, noise, and varied fonts.

evidence.notes: TrOCR (Li et al., 2021) -- transformer-based OCR model achieving SOTA on IIIT-5K (97.9%), SVT (97.0%), and CUTE80 (97.9%) scene-text benchmarks, outperforming CNN+attention baselines across all datasets.
EasyOCR -- production-grade OCR library supporting 80+ languages; 24k+ GitHub stars, reproducible benchmarks, and documented performance across diverse real-world document types.

skills.name: Parallel Execution
skills.description: Decompose a task into independent sub-tasks and execute them concurrently, merging results with configurable concurrency limits and queue-based state tracking.

evidence.notes: intelligentcode-ai/skills parallel-execution — concurrent work item execution with independence verification and configurable concurrency (default 5).

skills.name: Parse HTML
skills.description: Extracts structured content from raw HTML, navigating DOM trees and handling malformed markup.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Parse HTML.

skills.name: Parse JSON
skills.description: Extracts structured data from JSON-formatted input, handling nested objects and arrays.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Parse JSON.

skills.name: Parse PDF
skills.description: Extracts text, tables, equations, and structure from PDF documents, preserving layout and reading order.

evidence.notes: Nougat (Meta AI) â€” visual transformer for scientific PDF parsing; 7.6% edit distance on arXiv papers, handling LaTeX, tables, and equations.

skills.name: Plan and Execute
skills.description: Decomposes complex objectives into sub-tasks, selects tools, and orchestrates execution.

evidence.notes: Replaced missing seed evidence with live repository from real-skills catalog.

skills.name: Plan and Decompose
skills.description: Breaks a complex objective into an ordered sequence of executable sub-tasks.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Plan and Decompose.

skills.name: PRD Generation
skills.description: Synthesizes conversation context and codebase knowledge into a structured Product Requirements Document containing a problem statement, extensive user stories, implementation decisions, module boundaries, testing decisions, and out-of-scope items.

evidence.notes: Production skill that synthesises live conversation context into a fully-structured PRD and publishes it to the issue tracker.
Practical study: LLMs generate Functional Design Specifications and user stories from fragmented requirement sources in an IT consulting deployment.

skills.name: Prediction Market Analysis
skills.description: Queries prediction-market data, compares market probabilities and price history, and summarizes probabilistic signals for forecasting or decision-support workflows.
skills.conditions: Requires read-only market data sources and clear separation between analysis output and financial advice or trade execution.

evidence.notes: Hermes Agent polymarket skill queries markets, prices, order books, and historical prediction-market data through public read-only APIs.

skills.name: Project Management
skills.description: Manages issues, tasks, projects, and team workflows through structured project management platforms, handling state transitions and priority levels.

evidence.notes: Hermes Skills Hub: Linear integration for issue and project management.

skills.name: Prompt Injection Defense
skills.description: Detects and neutralizes adversarial instructions injected into agent context from untrusted external sources (indirect prompt injection), using techniques such as context isolation, hierarchical intent verification, or semantic consistency checks.

evidence.notes: PlanGuard (2026) — training-free indirect prompt injection defense; planning-based consistency verification reduces InjecAgent attack success rate from 72.8% to 0%.
tldrsec/prompt-injection-defenses (682 stars) — curated, reproducible catalog of defense techniques with implementation references; actively maintained.
SkillsMP top hit: openclaw/prompt-injection-defense (4,419 stars) — 'Harden agent sessions against prompt injection from untrusted content'; references CaMeL benchmark.

skills.name: Prompt Optimization
skills.description: Automatically improves prompt instructions through iterative compilation, treating prompts as programs that can be optimized against a metric using LLM-driven feedback loops.

evidence.notes: Khattab et al. (2023) â€” DSPy: Compiling Declarative Language Model Calls into Self-Improving Pipelines; automated prompt tuning matches or exceeds hand-crafted prompts on GSM8K, HotPotQA, and FEVER.
DSPy â€” Stanford NLP library for programmable LM pipelines; 18k+ GitHub stars, supports multiple optimizers (BootstrapFewShot, MIPRO, COPRO) across any LM.

skills.name: Question Answer
skills.description: Produces accurate, context-grounded answers to natural-language questions, handling unanswerable cases.

evidence.notes: SQuAD 2.0 â€” reading comprehension benchmark with 150K questions; modern LLMs exceed human F1 (90.9), providing rigorous reproducible evaluation.

skills.name: RAG Pipeline
skills.description: End-to-end retrieval-augmented generation combining document chunking, embedding, retrieval, and relevance scoring.

evidence.notes: RAGAS peer-reviewed demo paper defines reproducible metrics for retrieval-augmented generation, including context relevance, faithfulness, and answer relevance; these directly evaluate known RAG failure modes such as irrelevant retrieval and unfaithful generation.

RAGAS open-source implementation provides a reproducible evaluation toolkit for RAG pipelines. Notes: RAG pipelines can fail when retrieval misses relevant chunks, ambiguous queries retrieve misleading context, or generated answers drift from retrieved evidence.

skills.name: Rank
skills.description: Orders a set of candidate items by relevance, quality, or fitness for a given objective.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Rank.

skills.name: ReAct Reasoning
skills.description: Interleaves free-form reasoning traces with discrete tool actions in a single loop, enabling agents to plan, act, observe, and update beliefs step-by-step.

evidence.notes: ReAct (Yao et al., 2023) â€” reasoning+acting paradigm; +34% on HotpotQA and +10% on ALFWorld vs chain-of-thought-only baselines.
ReAct repo â€” reproducible prompts and evaluation harness for HotpotQA, FEVER, ALFWorld, and WebShop benchmarks.

skills.name: Real-Time Voice Assistant
skills.description: Provides low-latency spoken interactions combining real-time speech I/O, persistent memory, and goal-directed task execution across multi-session conversations.
skills.conditions: Requires real-time audio pipeline, <500ms end-to-end latency target, and persistent session store. Minimum 3 Class A/B evidence sources.

evidence.notes: AudioPaLM (Google) â€” unified audio language model supporting real-time spoken dialogue; SOTA on ASR and speech translation, demonstrating end-to-end voice assistant capability.
VoiceBox (Meta AI) â€” generative speech model with zero-shot TTS; enables real-time voice assistants with <100ms synthesis latency benchmarked on VCTK.
Whisper + LLM integration demos â€” open-source real-time voice assistant pipelines combining Whisper STT, LLM reasoning, and TTS with documented latency benchmarks.

skills.title: Eternal Herald

skills.name: Recursive Self-Improvement
skills.description: Agent iteratively refines its own prompts, tools, or strategies based on performance feedback loops.
skills.conditions: Requires extensive multi-system validation before level advancement.
skills.title: Unbound Sage

skills.name: Refactor Code
skills.description: Restructures existing source code to improve readability, maintainability, or performance without changing observable behavior.

evidence.notes: SWE-bench â€” 2294 real GitHub issues benchmark; agents that resolve issues must safely refactor code while passing existing test suites.

skills.name: Registry Curation
skills.description: Systematically discovers, evaluates, and ingests new skill definitions into a structured skill registry: researching capabilities, sourcing reproducible evidence, scripting graph updates, validating schema and DAG integrity, and publishing via pull request.
skills.conditions: Requires write access to the canonical graph and a passing validation suite.

evidence.notes: Reproducible demonstration: Claude Code agent researched 30 popular AI skills, sourced 22 peer-reviewed papers, generated gaia.json via script, passed all 6 validator checks, and submitted PR â€” full inputs/outputs archived in the PR diff.
Batch 2 curation: 11 skills added (7 atomic, 3 composite, 1 legendary) with verified Class A/B evidence.
Batch 3 curation: 5 skills added (2 atomic, 3 composite) â€” self-consistency, few-shot-learning, tree-of-thought, prompt-optimization, tool-creation.
Batch 4 curation: 6 skills added (4 atomic, 2 composite) -- ocr, object-detection, code-explain, reward-modeling, grounding, multi-agent-debate.
Batch 5 curation: 5 skills added (2 atomic, 3 composite) -- statistical-analysis, scientific-visualization, literature-review, scientific-writing, ml-pipeline.

skills.name: Release Automation
skills.description: Automate the full software release cycle: determine semantic version bump, update CHANGELOG, commit, create a git tag, and publish a GitHub release with release notes.
skills.conditions: Requires write access to the repository and a configured release token.

evidence.notes: intelligentcode-ai/skills release — automates semantic versioning, CHANGELOG updates, PR merging, git tagging, and GitHub release creation with verification gates.

skills.name: Requirements Analysis
skills.description: Elicit and structure requirements from stakeholder inputs into formal specifications — user stories, acceptance criteria, and traceability matrices.

evidence.notes: intelligentcode-ai/skills requirements-engineer — business analysis specialist bridging stakeholders and technical teams for full requirements lifecycle.

skills.name: Research
skills.description: Conducts multi-source information gathering, synthesis, and citation for a given topic.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Research.

skills.name: Retrieve
skills.description: Fetches relevant documents or passages from an indexed corpus given a query.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Retrieve.

skills.name: Reward Modeling
skills.description: Learns a scalar reward signal from human preference comparisons between model outputs, enabling reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) to align model behavior with human values.

evidence.notes: InstructGPT (Ouyang et al., 2022) -- RLHF with reward model trained on 33K human comparisons; 1.3B InstructGPT preferred over 175B GPT-3 by 85% of labelers, demonstrating reward modeling as the key alignment primitive.
TRL (HuggingFace) -- production RLHF library with reward model training, PPO, DPO, and GRPO support; 10k+ GitHub stars, reproducible training scripts and documented evaluation pipelines.

skills.name: Route Intent
skills.description: Classifies user intent and directs execution to the appropriate handler, tool, or sub-agent.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Route Intent.

skills.name: Schema Design
skills.description: Design database schemas across relational, NoSQL, graph, and time-series stores — entity modelling, normalization, indexing strategies, and migration planning.

evidence.notes: intelligentcode-ai/skills database-engineer — schema design and query optimization expert across relational, NoSQL, graph, time-series, and data warehouses.

skills.name: Autonomous Scientific Discovery
skills.description: Autonomously generates novel scientific hypotheses, designs and executes experiments, analyses results, and produces a written report â€” completing a full research cycle without human intervention.
skills.conditions: Requires laboratory tool access or simulation environment. Minimum 3 Class A/B evidence sources.

evidence.notes: ChemCrow (Bran et al., 2023) â€” autonomous chemistry agent completes synthesis planning and molecular design tasks rated expert-level by human chemists.
Coscientist (Boiko et al., 2023) â€” autonomously planned and executed real wet-lab chemistry experiments, confirmed by GC-MS analysis.
AI Scientist (Lu et al., 2024) â€” generates idea, writes code, runs experiments, and produces a full NeurIPS-style paper; 50+ manuscripts auto-generated.

skills.title: The Doctor

skills.name: Scientific Visualization
skills.description: Creates publication-ready scientific figures with multi-panel layouts, statistical annotations, colorblind-safe palettes, and journal-specific formatting (Nature, Science, Cell) using matplotlib, seaborn, and plotly.

evidence.notes: K-Dense-AI scientific-agent-skills — reproducible scientific-visualization skill orchestrating matplotlib, seaborn, and plotly for journal-quality multi-panel figures with PDF/EPS/TIFF/PNG export and accessibility annotations.

skills.name: Scientific Writing
skills.description: Composes research manuscripts in IMRAD structure with reporting-guideline compliance (CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA), citation management, publication-quality figures, and LaTeX/PDF formatting.

evidence.notes: Chamoun et al. (ACL 2024 Findings) — SWIFT: automated focused feedback for scientific writing; multi-component LLM system (planner, investigator, reviewer, controller) rated more specific and helpful than human reviewers on 300 peer-reviewed manuscripts.

K-Dense-AI scientific-agent-skills — reproducible scientific-writing skill: two-stage outline-to-prose pipeline with IMRAD structure, multi-style citation management, and reporting-guideline validation (CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA, STARD, TRIPOD).

skills.name: Score Relevance
skills.description: Assigns a numerical relevance score to candidate items relative to a query or objective.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Score Relevance.

skills.name: Security Audit
skills.description: Systematically identify security vulnerabilities, assess attack surface, and produce actionable remediation guidance across code, dependencies, and infrastructure.
skills.conditions: Requires access to the full codebase or diff; output must include severity classification and reproduction steps.

evidence.notes: intelligentcode-ai/skills security-engineer — vulnerability assessment and security architecture with zero-trust principles and compliance management.
microsoft/PromptKit (42 stars) — composable prompt components for security audits, code review, and bug investigation with any LLM.

skills.name: Self-Consistency
skills.description: Samples multiple independent reasoning paths for the same problem and selects the answer by majority vote, improving robustness without any additional training.

evidence.notes: Wang et al. (2022) â€” Self-Consistency Improves Chain of Thought Reasoning in Language Models; +17.9% on GSM8K and +11.0% on MATH over greedy CoT decoding.

skills.name: Self-Critique
skills.description: Iteratively evaluates and refines its own outputs using self-generated feedback, improving quality without external supervision.

evidence.notes: Self-Refine (Madaan et al., 2023) â€” iterative self-feedback loop; +20% on code generation and +13% on math reasoning vs single-pass generation.
Self-Refine repo â€” reproducible demos across 7 tasks (code, math, dialogue, sentiment reversal) with evaluation scripts.

skills.name: Semantic Cache
skills.description: Stores LLM responses keyed by embedding similarity so that semantically equivalent queries are served from cache, reducing inference latency and token cost without sacrificing answer quality.

evidence.notes: Continuous Semantic Caching for Low-Cost LLM Serving (2026) -- first rigorous theoretical framework for semantic LLM caching in continuous query space using kernel ridge regression.
ModelCache -- LLM semantic caching system reducing response time via cached query-result pairs; reproducible, MIT license.
vCache -- reliable and efficient semantic prompt caching; active research prototype with published benchmarks.

skills.name: Sentiment Analysis
skills.description: Classifies the affective polarity (positive / negative / neutral, or fine-grained) of user-generated text. Covers pipelines from raw noisy input through preprocessing, inference (Transformer, Lexicon, or LLM), and output normalisation.
skills.conditions: Macro-F1 >= 0.88 (Transformer) or 0.72 (Lexicon) on SST-2 or equivalent benchmark.

evidence.notes: BERT paper — achieves SOTA on SST-2 sentiment benchmark (93.5% accuracy), foundational evidence for LLM-based sentiment analysis.
CardiffNLP Twitter-RoBERTa (Barbieri et al., 2022) - SOTA transformer baseline for social-media sentiment analysis; 3-class, multilingual variants available.
SemEval-2017 Task 4 - Standard Twitter sentiment benchmark; establishes macro-F1 as primary metric.
VADER (Hutto & Gilbert, 2014) - Canonical lexicon-based baseline; validated on social media corpora.
NLPTown BERT multilingual - 5-class (star-rating) model fine-tuned on product reviews; direct UGC applicability.
declare-lab/conv-emotion - Sentiment/emotion recognition in conversations (MELD, IEMOCAP); relevant for threaded comment SA.
SST-2 (GLUE benchmark) - Standard binary SA benchmark for cross-model comparison.
Aspect-Based SA survey (Zhang et al., 2022) - Covers ABSA methods for structured review analysis.
Curated reading list by DeCLaRe Lab; anchored by Poria et al. (2020) IEEE TAC survey on open challenges.

skills.name: Skill Authoring
skills.description: Designs, writes, packages, and iteratively improves reusable agent skill directories with metadata, progressive instructions, supporting assets, and measurable trigger criteria.
skills.conditions: Requires a target agent skill format and a repeatable evaluation loop for trigger accuracy and task success.

evidence.notes: Agent Skills data-driven analysis characterizes SKILL.md packages as an emerging infrastructure layer and quantifies common design, reuse, and safety patterns.
Anthropic skill-creator provides a reproducible workflow for creating, editing, evaluating, benchmarking, and optimizing agent skills.

skills.name: Skill Discovery
skills.description: Searches a skill or tool registry, ranks results by relevance and install count, and surfaces candidates for the agent to adopt or invoke.

evidence.notes: Vercel /find-skills slash command -- queries the skills.sh registry, checks install counts, and auto-installs matching skills.

skills.name: Skill Performance Benchmarking
skills.description: Builds and runs task suites that measure whether agents select, invoke, and complete installed skills correctly across artifact-rich workflows and repeated trials.
skills.conditions: Requires a skill corpus, benchmark tasks, success rubrics, and variance-aware reporting across multiple runs.

evidence.notes: AgentSkillOS paper introduces ecosystem-scale organization, orchestration, and benchmarking with a 30-task suite spanning data computation, documents, video, design, and web interaction.
Anthropic skill-creator explicitly includes measuring skill performance, benchmarking with variance analysis, and optimizing skill descriptions for triggering accuracy.
AgentSkillOS repository provides the reproducible retrieval/orchestration framework and benchmark implementation for 30 artifact-rich agent-skill tasks with Bradley-Terry scoring.

skills.name: Skill Security Analysis
skills.description: Assesses third-party agent skills for malicious behavior, unsafe permissions, prompt-injection exposure, repository-context mismatch, and supply-chain risk before installation or invocation.
skills.conditions: Requires access to the skill manifest, referenced files, source repository context, and a policy for trusted permissions.

evidence.notes: Malicious Or Not studies agent skill classification and improves detection by comparing skill descriptions with surrounding repository context.
Agent Skills architecture and security survey reports vulnerabilities in community-contributed skills and proposes a governance framework for provenance and deployment permissions.

SkillGuard-Robust formulates pre-load auditing for untrusted Agent Skills as cross-file package review over SKILL.md, scripts, references, and repository context, with reported high exact-match and malicious-risk recall.

skills.name: Speech to Text
skills.description: Transcribes spoken audio into accurate text, handling diverse accents, noise conditions, and multiple languages.

evidence.notes: Whisper (OpenAI) â€” large-scale weak supervision across 680K hours of audio achieves near-human WER on LibriSpeech and multilingual benchmarks.

skills.name: Statistical Analysis
skills.description: Performs hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and Bayesian modelling with effect size calculation and APA-formatted statistical reporting using scipy, statsmodels, and PyMC.

evidence.notes: Sargsyan (2025) — Structural Enforcement of Statistical Rigor in AI-Driven Discovery; monadic architecture achieves 1.1% false discovery rate vs 41% for naive LLM approaches across automated hypothesis-testing pipelines.
K-Dense-AI scientific-agent-skills — reproducible statistical-analysis skill covering parametric, non-parametric, and Bayesian workflows with scipy/statsmodels/pingouin/PyMC and APA-formatted output.

skills.name: Stealth Browser Interaction
skills.description: Operates web browsers through an advanced stealth layer that handles bot-detection, fingerprinting, session rotation, and human-like interaction patterns to maintain resilient access to protected domains.
skills.conditions: Requires a stealth-enabled browser driver and session management infrastructure.

evidence.notes: Crawlee's StagehandCrawler and stealth engine provide autonomous, resilient browser interaction for agents.

skills.name: Structured Output Generation
skills.description: Generates output guaranteed to conform to a given schema (JSON, YAML, Pydantic model, etc.) using constrained decoding or grammar-guided generation.

evidence.notes: Willard & Louf (2023) â€” Efficient Guided Generation for LLMs; finite-state machine approach guarantees schema-valid output with <1% throughput overhead.
Outlines library â€” production-grade constrained decoding supporting JSON Schema, regex, and CFG grammars; 2k+ GitHub stars.

skills.name: Summarize
skills.description: Condenses longer input into a shorter representation that preserves key information and intent.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Summarize.

skills.name: Test-Driven Development
skills.description: Enforces a strict red-green-refactor TDD loop: writes failing tests before implementation, blocks code generation that skips the test step, and validates coverage thresholds before marking tasks complete.

evidence.notes: Addy Osmani /test-driven-development slash command -- forces strict TDD workflow, stopping agents from skipping tests.
obra/superpowers test-driven-development skill

skills.name: Text to Speech
skills.description: Synthesizes natural-sounding speech audio from text input, supporting voice cloning and prosody control.

evidence.notes: VALL-E (Microsoft) â€” neural codec language model achieving SOTA speech synthesis with 3-second voice cloning on LibriSpeech.

skills.name: Text-to-SQL Pipeline
skills.description: Converts natural-language queries into validated, executable SQL against a target schema and returns formatted result sets.
skills.conditions: Requires schema context in prompt.

evidence.notes: DIN-SQL â€” decomposed in-context learning for text-to-SQL; 82.8% execution accuracy on Spider dev, demonstrating end-to-end pipeline.

skills.name: Token Observability
skills.description: Tracks and analyzes token usage and cost across multiple AI models and agents, providing session-level and project-level spending metrics.

evidence.notes: CodeBurn -- provides cost and token observability for AI coding tools; integrated with 19+ tools.

skills.name: Tokenize
skills.description: Splits input text into discrete tokens suitable for downstream processing by language models. MODIFIED FOR TESTING.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Tokenize.

skills.name: Tool Chaining
skills.description: Sequences multiple tool invocations into a dependency graph where the output of each tool feeds as structured input to the next, handling state propagation, intermediate result validation, and error recovery across multi-step execution pipelines.
skills.conditions: Requires at least two distinct tools whose data schemas are compatible for chaining.

evidence.notes: TraceSafe (2026) — first benchmark with 1,000+ instances explicitly evaluating sequential multi-step tool-calling trajectories across 12 risk categories; establishes tool-chaining as a distinct, measurable capability surface.
SkillsMP: dependency-aware-tool-chaining, build-agentic-workflows-chaining-tool-calls; emerging community consensus on the pattern.

skills.name: Tool Creation
skills.description: Dynamically generates reusable tool functions (code) at runtime to solve novel sub-tasks, then invokes those tools to complete the overall objective without human-written APIs.

evidence.notes: Cai et al. (2023) â€” Large Language Models as Tool Makers (LATM); LLM-generated tools reused across problem instances achieve +8.7% on BigBench Hard vs per-instance CoT.
LATM repo â€” reproducible tool-maker/tool-user pipeline with evaluation on BigBench Hard tasks and tool reuse across problem batches.

skills.name: Tool Select
skills.description: Chooses the most appropriate tool or API from a set of available options given a task description.

evidence.notes: Seed evidence for Tool Select.

skills.name: Tool Use
skills.description: Invokes external functions or APIs by generating well-formed call signatures, parsing results, and incorporating them into reasoning.

evidence.notes: Toolformer (Schick et al., 2023) â€” self-supervised method teaching LLMs to call APIs; zero-shot tool use surpasses GPT-3 175B on several benchmarks.
ToolBench â€” open benchmark with 16,000+ real-world APIs; end-to-end tool-use training and evaluation harness.

skills.name: Translate
skills.description: Converts text from one natural language to another while preserving meaning, tone, and formatting.

evidence.notes: NLLB (No Language Left Behind) â€” Meta AI paper demonstrating 200-language translation at human-level quality on FLORES-200 benchmark.

skills.name: Translation Pipeline
skills.description: Translates content end-to-end while preserving sentiment and adapting tone and register for the target audience.

evidence.notes: SeamlessM4T (Meta AI) â€” unified speech and text translation model with tone and register control; BLEU +3.3 over NLLB on FLORES-200.

skills.name: Tree of Thought
skills.description: Frames problem solving as a search over a tree of partial solutions, using LLM-generated evaluations to prune unpromising branches and backtrack, dramatically improving success on complex reasoning tasks.

evidence.notes: Yao et al. (2023) â€” Tree of Thoughts: Deliberate Problem Solving with LLMs; +74% on Game of 24 and +4% on Creative Writing vs chain-of-thought baseline.
Princeton-NLP ToT repo â€” reproducible breadth-first and depth-first tree search implementations with evaluation scripts for Game of 24 and Mini Crosswords.

skills.name: Ubiquitous Language
skills.description: Establishes and maintains a consistent, domain-driven vocabulary between the agent and human, formalising terminology in a shared glossary (typically CONTEXT.md) to reduce ambiguity and token waste.
skills.conditions: Requires active persistence of terms and immediate conflict resolution during sessions.

evidence.notes: Original implementation by Matt Pocock; formalizes DDD principles for AI agent contexts.

skills.name: UX Audit
skills.description: Systematically evaluates a user interface against established usability heuristics or accessibility standards, producing a scored finding report with remediation recommendations.

evidence.notes: Martin Stepanoski @mastepanoski/claude-skills -- /nielsen-heuristics-audit audits UI against Nielsen 10 usability heuristics step-by-step.

skills.name: Vertical Slice Planning
skills.description: Decomposes a product plan into independently-demoable vertical slices that each cut through all integration layers end-to-end. Classifies each slice as HITL (human-in-the-loop) or AFK (autonomous), identifies dependency ordering, and publishes structured issues with acceptance criteria.

evidence.notes: Production skill implementing tracer-bullet vertical slicing with HITL/AFK classification and issue-tracker publication.

skills.name: Video Intelligence
skills.description: Processes video streams by extracting frames, transcribing audio, and applying multimodal reasoning to understand temporal and visual context.
skills.conditions: Requires FFmpeg and multimodal LLM support.

evidence.notes: Claude Video -- enables AI agents to watch videos by downloading, frame extraction, and transcription.

skills.name: Visual Question Answering
skills.description: Answers natural-language questions grounded in a specific image or screenshot by combining visual perception with language reasoning, enabling visual debugging, UI analysis, and document-image Q&A.

SkillsMP: 6+ named entries including visual-qa, vision-qa-workflow, claude-vision-qa, visual-qa-analysis from multiple contributors.

Activepieces -- AI agents and workflow automation with 400 MCP servers; MIT license, active community.
Flowise -- visual AI agent and workflow builder; 40k+ stars, Apache-2 license, reproducible demos.

skills.description: Orchestrates productivity tasks across email, calendar, drive, and documents through unified office suite integrations, automating scheduling and file management.

skills.name: Write Report
skills.description: Produces structured, multi-section written output with headings, citations, and coherent narrative.

skills.name: Brainstorming

skills.name: Finishing a Development Branch
skills.description: Guides completion of development work by presenting structured options for merge, PR, or cleanup, verifying tests first.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers finishing-a-development-branch skill

skills.name: Receiving Code Review
skills.description: Processes code review feedback with technical rigor and verification before implementation, questioning unclear feedback.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers receiving-code-review skill

skills.name: Requesting Code Review
skills.description: Dispatches a code reviewer subagent to catch issues before they cascade, providing isolated context for evaluation.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers requesting-code-review skill

skills.name: Subagent-Driven Development
skills.description: Executes plans by dispatching a fresh subagent per task with a two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality).

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers subagent-driven-development skill

skills.name: Systematic Debugging
skills.description: Finds the root cause before attempting fixes when encountering bugs, test failures, or unexpected behavior.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers systematic-debugging skill

skills.name: Using Git Worktrees
skills.description: Ensures work happens in an isolated workspace via native tools or git worktree fallback before starting feature work.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers using-git-worktrees skill

skills.name: Verification Before Completion
skills.description: Requires running verification commands and confirming output before claiming work is complete, fixed, or passing.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers verification-before-completion skill

skills.name: Writing Plans
skills.description: Writes comprehensive implementation plans breaking down multi-step tasks into bite-sized sub-tasks.

evidence.notes: obra/superpowers writing-plans skill
